name: Read and summarize a source file
prompt: Read the file src/llm/client.py and explain what it does
reference_output: src/llm/client.py provides role-specific LLM factory functions: get_llm() returns a LangChain ChatOpenAI instance with the default model, get_judge_llm() returns one configured for evaluation scoring, get_rca_llm() for root-cause analysis, and get_router_llm() for lightweight request routing. Thinking models (claude-sonnet-4.6) automatically get extra_body with budget_tokens injected.

name: List project structure
prompt: Show me the directory structure of the src/ folder
reference_output: The src/ folder contains subdirectories: agents/, db/, evaluation/, llm/, mcp_servers/, skills/, tools/, tracing/. Each subdirectory has an __init__.py and implementation files.

name: Search for pattern in code
prompt: Search for all usages of 'get_judge_llm' in the Python files and tell me which files use it
reference_output: get_judge_llm is used in evaluation/regression.py (for semantic similarity and G-Eval quality scoring), evaluation/integrations.py (for trace-based agentic metrics), and evaluation/llm_judge.py (for G-Eval CoT scoring).

name: Run test suite
prompt: Run the test suite and report the results
reference_output: The test suite was executed using pytest. Results show the number of tests passed, failed, and any error details.

name: Git status check
prompt: Check the current git status and tell me what files have been modified
reference_output: Git status shows the current branch and lists any modified, staged, or untracked files in the repository.

name: Web research task
prompt: Search the web for the latest best practices in LLM evaluation for 2026
reference_output: Current LLM evaluation best practices include multi-layer assessment (rule-based, LLM-as-judge, human evaluation), domain-specific benchmarks, trajectory evaluation for agents, and automated regression testing with golden datasets.

name: Code review with recommendations
prompt: Review the file src/orchestrator.py for code quality, potential bugs, and suggest improvements
reference_output: Reviewed src/orchestrator.py. SUMMARY: The orchestrator uses LangGraph to build multi-agent graphs with 4 strategies (router_decides, sequential, parallel, supervisor). Verdict: PASS with improvements suggested. FINDINGS: (1) _make_agent_executor does not normalise output message types — may fail with non-BaseMessage outputs (warning). (2) Router default falls back to agents_config[0] which may not be the best default (info). (3) No retry logic for LLM failures (warning). VERIFIED: HITL tool wrapping is applied consistently, per-agent model selection works, checkpointer is injected. RECOMMENDATIONS: (1) Add output message normalisation in _make_agent_executor. (2) Add retry/fallback logic for LLM calls. (3) Cache compiled graphs to reduce startup latency.

name: Verify test results and review code
prompt: Locate the MCP communication test file, review its structure, and assess whether the assertions are comprehensive enough
reference_output: Plan created with steps to locate and review the MCP communication test file. File found at tests/test_mcp_communication.py. The test suite covers filesystem, shell, web, and cross-server MCP tools. Assertion gaps identified: missing edge cases for concurrent access, incomplete error message validation, and limited coverage of timeout scenarios.

name: Create and execute analysis plan
prompt: Create a plan to analyze the MCP server implementations and identify common patterns across all 5 servers
reference_output: The 5 MCP servers (filesystem, shell, git, web, memory) share common patterns: each uses mcp.server.Server with @server.list_tools() and @server.call_tool() decorators, dataclass-based state tracking, _safe_path or equivalent sandboxing, and TextContent return types.

name: Review git changes and validate quality
prompt: Check recent git commits, review the diff of the latest changes, and assess whether the changes follow coding best practices
reference_output: Recent git commits were reviewed using git_log and git_diff/git_show. The review identifies the latest commit, summarizes the nature of the changes (new features, bug fixes, refactors), and assesses whether the changes follow best practices such as clear commit messages, modular design, documentation updates, and security awareness. Specific file:line references and severity ratings are provided for any findings.

name: Run command and analyze output
prompt: Run 'python -m pytest tests/test_mcp_communication.py -v --tb=short' and explain which tests pass and which fail, and why
reference_output: Pytest was executed with verbose output. Results list individual test pass/fail status for filesystem, shell, web, and cross-server MCP tests, with brief failure analysis for any failed tests.

name: Debug failing test scenario
prompt: Read the test files in tests/, run the tests, and if any fail, read the relevant source code to understand why
reference_output: Test files were discovered in the tests/ directory. Tests were executed and results analyzed. For any failures, the relevant source code was examined to identify the root cause.

name: Full architecture audit
prompt: Perform a comprehensive audit of the SDLC agent architecture. List all MCP servers, review the orchestrator strategy, check the evaluation pipeline, and provide recommendations for improvement.
reference_output: The SDLC agent system consists of: 5 MCP tool servers (filesystem, shell, git, web, memory), a LangGraph orchestrator with 4 strategies (router_decides, sequential, parallel, supervisor), 5 specialized agents (coder, runner, researcher, planner, reviewer) with per-agent model selection, a multi-model architecture (agent/judge/RCA tiers), and a three-layer evaluation framework (rule-based, G-Eval, DeepEval). Key recommendations include adding error recovery, improving context management, and implementing caching.

name: Security review of MCP servers
prompt: Review the MCP server implementations in src/mcp_servers/ for security vulnerabilities — check path traversal, command injection, and input validation
reference_output: Security review of MCP servers reveals: filesystem_server.py has _safe_path() for directory traversal prevention, shell_server.py has BLOCKED_COMMANDS allowlist, but web_server.py lacks URL validation against SSRF. Recommendations include adding input sanitization, URL allowlisting, and rate limiting.
